commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a first-party `ovh` SSH-lease provider with OVH config, signed-request client code, lifecycle/cleanup handling, docs, tests, provider registration, and an opt-in live smoke script.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds a new provider rather than fixing a current-main bug. Current-main inspection confirms there is no named OVH provider today.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the branch adds a new first-party cloud provider with missing live proof and an unresolved product decision; there is no narrow automation repair to queue.

target.title: feat: add OVHcloud Public Cloud provider
target.body:
Closes #278

Issue context: https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/278 requests an OVHcloud Public Cloud provider. The issue currently asks for a maintainer product decision between using Crabbox's shipped `external` provider route and accepting a first-party built-in OVH adapter. This PR proposes the first-party `ovh` provider route for review.

### Summary

- Adds an `ovh` provider for OVHcloud Public Cloud compute using Crabbox's direct SSH lease model.
- Keeps OVH-specific lifecycle, signed-request auth, image/flavor defaults, and cleanup behavior behind the provider adapter boundary.
- Uses OVH signed-request authentication with `/auth/time` server-time offset handling.
- Stores OVH ownership in local Crabbox lease claims and validates live instance identity before claim-backed touch, cleanup, and Tailscale metadata updates.
- Documents provider configuration and credential shape without placing secrets on the command line or in committed config examples.
- Includes deterministic tests for provider registration, CLI/config behavior, client signing, lifecycle recovery, claim preservation, and opt-in live-smoke behavior.

### Product Decision Framing

The repository already supports an `external` provider path, and the issue review correctly calls out that as an alternative. This PR is intentionally framed as the first-party option: if maintainers prefer OVH to prove out externally first, this branch can be treated as implementation evidence rather than merged as-is.

Recommended maintainer decision for this PR:

- Accept first-party ownership of a bundled `ovh` provider if Crabbox wants named OVH UX, direct configuration, and built-in support.
- Otherwise, ask for a separate external-provider OVH prototype and keep this PR closed or deferred until first-party ownership is accepted.

### Verification

Rebased onto current upstream `origin/main` commit `f4854493564cfff1600311b03a7e1c5b81d2d1fa`.

Current PR head: `198d4bb5686754eccd13424e0527f740a232c0f1`.

```text
PASS git diff --check
PASS go test ./internal/providers/ovh ./internal/providers/all ./internal/cli ./cmd/crabbox
PASS go test -race ./internal/providers/ovh ./internal/providers/all ./internal/cli
PASS go vet ./...
PASS node --test scripts/live-ovh-smoke.test.js
PASS node scripts/check-docs-links.mjs
PASS node scripts/check-command-docs.mjs
PASS node scripts/generate-provider-matrix.mjs --check
PASS ~/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
```

Optional live smoke classification:

```text
PASS scripts/live-ovh-smoke.sh
classification=environment_blocked reason=CRABBOX_LIVE_not_enabled
```

No live OVH resource mutation was attempted.

### Review Fixes Already Addressed

```text
ADDRESSED autoreview: unsupported OVH instance create labels removed; OVH ownership is local-claim backed.
ADDRESSED autoreview: escaped OVH API path segments no longer pass through path normalization.
ADDRESSED autoreview: status --wait ReadyProbe resolution returns an SSH target.
ADDRESSED autoreview: key-only ambiguous-create recovery claims can release owned OVH SSH keys.
ADDRESSED autoreview: GET /cloud/project decodes OVH's real string-array response shape.
ADDRESSED autoreview: signed OVH requests use cached /auth/time server offset instead of raw local time.
ADDRESSED autoreview: OVH touch preserves local claim metadata when live OVH labels are sparse.
ADDRESSED autoreview: OVH persists direct Tailscale metadata into local lease claims.
CLEAN final autoreview: no accepted/actionable findings reported.
```

### Credential And Privacy Notes

- OVH application secrets, consumer keys, API keys, project-private values, local usernames, local absolute paths, and copied local credential files are intentionally omitted.
- Secret OVH values should remain environment-only.
- Public docs describe variable names and configuration shape, not real values.
